source: Faz11 gap re-coverage — 3 Claude general-purpose sub-agents (frontend components / scripts / orchestration), reliably read the slices the ollamas fleet failed to read. Tier-1 verified the CRITICALs; remaining = SUSPECTED (agent-evidenced, file:line, Tier-1 spot-verify pending).

findings.name: rankEfficient disqualifies valid champions (rounded correctRatio gate)
findings.symptom: aggregate() stores correctRatio = round(corr/n) where round=Math.round(n*10)/10 (1 decimal, bench.ts:138). rankEfficient gates `if (a.correctRatio <= 0.9) continue` (119). So a model 85-94.9% correct rounds to 0.9 and is EXCLUDED as champion; only >=95% (rounds to 1.0) survive. The 'most efficient correct model' selection — bench's core purpose, feeding benchprompt champion + MODEL_SELECTION — silently drops strong models or picks a slower one / none.
findings.fix: Rank on the RAW unrounded ratio (keep a correctRatioRaw), or change the gate to `< 0.9` AND don't round the value used for gating.

findings.name: withLock runs fn() even when lock NOT acquired
findings.symptom: withLock retries mkdir-lock 50x; if still not held (another writer holds a non-stale lock), it falls through to `try { return fn() } finally { if (held) rmdir }` (135-139) — fn() runs UNCONDITIONALLY, so the guarded write (claim append) executes without the lock -> concurrent duplicate claims for the same lane|version (exactly the ERR-ORCH-013 the lock exists to prevent).
findings.fix: After the loop: `if (!held) throw new Error('claim lock not acquired');` before calling fn().
findings.evidence: Code claims.ts:116-139 — held stays false after 50 fails yet fn() is called at :136.

findings.name: app.health CRITICAL check hits wrong path (CLAIMED) -> always FAIL
findings.symptom: Agent claimed GET ${APP}/health 404s because the server only mounts /api/health. FALSE: live curl http://127.0.0.1:8099/health -> 200 (the server DOES expose a bare /health alias in addition to /api/health). The monitor check works.
findings.fix: (none — not a bug)
findings.evidence: Tier-1 reproduce: curl /health -> 200 AND curl /api/health -> 200 on live :8099. Agent assumed no bare /health; there is one.

findings.name: dedupe fingerprint misses stale-test:/redlane: prefixes
findings.symptom: fp() prefix-strip regex doesn't include the `stale-test:`/`redlane:` prefixes that qualityToReqs emits (fuse.ts:66,83) -> same-lane requirements don't merge -> duplicate/contradictory entries (e.g. gate:backend + stale-test:backend both survive) in REQUIREMENTS.
findings.fix: Add stale-test|redlane to the prefix alternation in fp().

findings.name: version-source gate resolves wrong roadmap file (silent no-op)
findings.symptom: findFile (shared.ts breadth-first) matches root COLAB_ROADMAP.md for /roadmap.*\.md$/i before descending to orchestration/ROADMAP_ORCHESTRATION.md -> declaredVersion='' -> checkVersionSources emits 0 rows every run; the HARD version-drift gate never actually checks. (plan-next.ts:232 shares the same wrong-file resolution.)
findings.fix: Anchor the regex (e.g. prefer orchestration/ROADMAP_ORCHESTRATION.md) or pass an explicit path.

findings.name: brittle relative TSX path breaks in worktree
findings.symptom: TSX resolved via ../../../ollamas/node_modules (relative) instead of ANCHOR; run from .claude/worktrees/<wt>/orchestration/bin it resolves a missing path -> every execJson throws -> runSource()=null -> heartbeat tick silently aborts.
findings.fix: const TSX = join(ANCHOR, 'node_modules','.bin','tsx') (as autopilot/doctor do).
findings.evidence: Agent code-read heartbeat.ts:33; fuse.ts:29 shares the class but resolves in this checkout.

findings.name: conduct RED-tier exit-1 drops next-action
findings.symptom: conduct exits 1 on a normal RED-tier gate; runStep marks ok:false and summarizeAutopilot sets next-action to '—' exactly when an action exists -> AUTOPILOT.md hides the next action on RED.
findings.fix: Tolerate conduct exit-1 (capture stdout) like fuse.ts:42, then read CONDUCTOR.md.

findings.name: renewClaim no ownership/status check (LWW clobber)
findings.symptom: renewClaim appends status:'claimed',ts:now for any tab with no ownership/state guard -> tab B renewing A's lane wins foldClaims LWW -> A silently loses the claim; can also revive done/released claims.
findings.fix: Only renew if the active claim's tab===opts.tab and state not done/released.

findings.name: version-source compares incompatible numbering schemes
findings.symptom: When the roadmap DOES resolve, it compares vO/Faz numbering (roadmapCurrent 'vO16'->16) vs git tag 'v1.22.1'->1 -> false HARD drift -> exit 1 (currently masked only because O-004 makes roadmapCurrent='').
findings.fix: Compare only same-scheme sources; skip git-tag when roadmap uses vO/Faz.

findings.name: --search multi-word query truncated to first word
findings.symptom: target = args.find(non-flag) returns only the first token; call('/search',{query:target}) searches one word for a multi-word query.
findings.fix: Build query from args.slice(searchIdx+1).join(' ').

findings.name: Browser Search E2E green-lights a simulation
findings.symptom: browser_search is in tools.json but unhandled in executeTool -> falls to localExecution returning {executed:true,output:'Simulation Success'}; .success true -> the E2E asserts success on a no-op.
findings.fix: Route browser_search to a real impl or drop the success claim.

findings.name: dead logActivity/logFailure
findings.symptom: logActivity + logFailure defined but never called (real logging is inline console.log).
findings.fix: Delete both.

findings.name: have('ollama') PATH-only -> false hint for GUI Ollama
findings.symptom: have('ollama') only scans $PATH; Ollama.app (daemon-only, no CLI on PATH) yields a false 'install ollama' hint even when the daemon is up.
findings.fix: Probe daemon presence, not PATH, for the hint.

findings.name: addHook secret.slice on undefined
findings.symptom: addHook does r.secret.slice(0,16) unconditionally; if POST /api/saas/webhooks returns no secret (exists/validation/older server) -> TypeError -> webhook appears unsaved, error shown as 'Cannot read properties of undefined' not the real cause.
findings.fix: guard r.secret before slice/copy.

findings.name: write_file approval throws on null args
findings.symptom: On a write_file SSE step, setPendingApproval({path:parsed.args.path,...}); if args is null/absent (error/partial trace) -> throws in the SSE loop -> caught as 'Could not parse SSE', approval wizard never opens, write silently lost.
findings.fix: null-check parsed.args?.path before opening approval.

findings.name: error break exits inner loop only; later chunks overwrite fail status
findings.symptom: On data.error SSE, code break exits one chunk's line loop, not the stream; streamPost keeps consuming; later stage events setStages overwrite the 'fail' status back to running/done.
findings.fix: set an errored flag (or abort the controller) and ignore further stage updates.

findings.name: auto-load effect deps [] never refires on token paste
findings.symptom: useEffect(()=>{if(adminToken)refresh()},[]) runs once on mount; a new operator pasting the token later never auto-loads until manual Refresh.
findings.fix: depend on [adminToken] or refresh on token change.

findings.name: unguarded setState after unmount
findings.symptom: mount effect api.get('/api/cluster/capabilities') has no unmount guard; switching tabs before resolve -> setOrchestratorError after unmount (React warning / wasted set).
findings.fix: add mounted ref or AbortController (like useUsage/useLogbook).

findings.name: double round-trip when server root != prop
findings.symptom: fetchTree calls onPathChange(workspaceRoot) when root differs; effect dep [activePath] refires fetchTree -> a second round-trip on every mount where server root != prop.
findings.fix: compare and skip, or mount-only fetch.

findings.name: dead cpuHistory state (+ setState every 5s)
findings.symptom: cpuHistory computed/set each poll but never rendered -> pure dead state + a setState every 5s.
findings.fix: drop the state+effect or render it.
